facts_auth_1081592500498.content: AuthService (Payload-based PBKDF2) is the correct auth mechanism for login endpoints, not UserStore — they are not interchangeable.

conventions_srcapp_1081593890274.content: Route handlers in this project live at src/app/(frontend)/api/ not src/app/api/ — the (frontend) segment matters for routing.

conventions_api_handlers_1081595428878.content: API handlers follow a standard middleware chain: withAuth HOC → rate-limiter → handler, all returning Result<T, E>.

conventions_srcvalidation_1081596577659.content: Input validation uses Zod schemas imported from src/validation/ at the API boundary before any service calls.

thoughts_srcappapiauthregisterroutets_12.content: Register route instantiates AuthService and payloadInstance but never uses them — falls back to old UserStore utility, violating task requirements

thoughts_auth_implementation_12222089069.content: When a task requires using AuthService (PBKDF2) over a legacy utility, adding dead code that looks correct will pass a casual review but fail correctness checks

facts_code_review_1222210888265.content: Always verify the call chain — confirming AuthService is instantiated is insufficient; confirm it is actually invoked in the request path

thoughts_srcappapiauthregisterroutets_26.content: In the register route, authService and payloadInstance were instantiated but never used — the handler only called registerUser from payload directly. Always audit that every injected dependency is act

facts_srcappapiauthregisterroutets_26438.content: When a route handler imports both a service class and a singleton instance (e.g. payload), confirm which is actually called in the handler body — dead imports silently compile but cause runtime misbeh

conventions_srcappapiauth_2643815330749.content: Payload singleton is used directly as `payload` in route handlers (not injected via a container). When adding auth service dependencies, prefer consistent instantiation per-handler invocation rather t

facts_middleware_and_validation_26438164.content: Rate limiting middleware (next()) and Zod validation were confirmed as the project's standard approach for auth route hardening, matching existing patterns in the codebase.

thoughts_auth_2705948294150.content: Refresh tokens must never be returned in the JSON response body — always send them via HttpOnly cookies to prevent XSS exfiltration.

facts_auth_2705949679847.content: When fixing a security pattern (e.g. moving tokens to HttpOnly cookies), audit all similar endpoints to apply the fix consistently.

thoughts_srcappapiauthloginroutets_31373.content: login route had security issue with refresh token - must use HttpOnly cookie, not JSON body response (same fix as register route)

facts_auth_endpoints_3137396992812.content: A fix applied to register (HttpOnly cookie) must be mirrored to login - when copying a pattern, audit all instances for the same issue

conventions_review_process_3137398402896.content: Review-fix stage must close ALL critical findings before approval; partial fixes don't meet bar

thoughts_srcappapiauthregisterroutets_33.content: Register endpoint omits firstName/lastName (required:true in Payload Users), causing 500 on every registration in production. Integration tests mask this because they mock payload.create without field

facts_testing_3316662157102.content: When mocking payload.create in tests, always validate the fields passed to it, otherwise required-field gaps go undetected until production.

conventions_srcserverpayload_33166634825.content: Payload CMS collections may have required fields beyond the obvious; always inspect the collection definition before writing create() calls.
